qa_pair.question: Scan the sample-project fixture for hardcoded secrets using inspectra_scan_secrets. How many distinct secret findings does the tool return? Answer with a number.

Run inspectra_check_todos on the sample-project fixture. The tool reports both TODO and FIXME markers across all source files. What is the total number of TODO + FIXME findings returned? Answer with a number.

Run inspectra_check_file_lengths on the sample-project fixture with a strict profile (warning threshold: 50, error threshold: 200). Which source file triggers the file-length finding? Answer with just the filename including extension, no directory path.

Use inspectra_detect_missing_tests on the sample-project fixture to find source files with no corresponding test file. The fixture has src/controllers/user.controller.ts, src/services/auth.service.ts, and src/utils/math-utils.ts (which has math-utils.test.ts). How many source files are reported as missing tests? Answer with a number.

Scan the sample-project fixture for hardcoded secrets with inspectra_scan_secrets. The auth.service.ts file contains an intentionally hardcoded API key. What is the secret token value that starts with "sk-"? Copy the exact token string.
Run inspectra_analyze_dependencies on the sample-project fixture. How many direct production dependencies (not devDependencies) are declared in its package.json? Answer with a number.

Run inspectra_check_todos on the sample-project fixture and examine the evidence snippets in each finding. All findings have rule "unresolved-todo", but the snippet text contains either "TODO" or "FIXME". How many findings have "FIXME" in their snippet? Answer with a number.

Perform a two-domain audit of the sample-project fixture: (1) run inspectra_scan_secrets to get security findings, (2) run inspectra_check_todos to get conventions findings, (3) use inspectra_score_findings to score each domain separately, (4) use inspectra_merge_domain_reports to merge both domain reports. In the consolidated report, how many total findings appear across both domains combined? Answer with a number.

Run inspectra_check_naming on the sample-project fixture with default settings. The fixture files use kebab-case naming with domain suffixes (user.controller.ts, auth.service.ts, math-utils.ts). Does the naming check produce zero findings (meaning all files comply)? Answer True or False.

Perform a three-domain audit of the sample-project fixture: security (scan_secrets on auth.service.ts), conventions (check_todos + check_file_lengths with warning:50 error:200), and tests (detect_missing_tests). Score each domain with inspectra_score_findings, then merge all three domain reports with inspectra_merge_domain_reports. The scoring system uses grades: A (90+), B (75+), C (60+), D (40+), F (below 40). What letter grade does the overall consolidated report receive? Answer A, B, C, D, or F.
